subject: Physics
title: Laplace's Demon
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Laplace's Demon.

hook.text: Laplace imagined an intellect that knows every particle's position and velocity—it could predict the entire future.

buildup.text: In classical mechanics, the universe is deterministic. Given perfect initial conditions, everything that follows is fixed.

discovery.text: Laplace's demon would know the past and future of every atom. Free will would be an illusion.

twist.text: Quantum mechanics destroyed the demon. Heisenberg's uncertainty principle says you can't know position and velocity precisely.

climax.text: Even chaos theory shows that tiny measurement errors grow exponentially, making long-term prediction impossible.

punchline.text: A perfect predictor killed by uncertainty. The future isn't written.

quiz.question: What destroyed the concept of Laplace's demon?
options: Quantum mechanics and the Heisenberg uncertainty principle
